articles.title: PACS IMPAX/EI — JVM Heap Exhaustion Recovery

sections.title: Detection Pattern
steps: Prometheus alert: heap_used_ratio > 0.95 on pacs-app-prod-* namespace
DICOM receiver thread count drops below 8 (normal: 24-32)
OOM kill events appear in pod logs — EtchRecipeProcessor or DicomReceiverThread
Image retrieval failure rate increases — monitored via Datadog APM

sections.title: Immediate Relief
steps: Capture heap dump BEFORE restart: kubectl exec pacs-app-prod-01 -- jmap -dump:format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof
Rolling restart: kubectl rollout restart deployment/pacs-app --strategy=RollingUpdate
Validate pod health before each rollover — check heap < 20% before proceeding
Apply interim config patch: -Xmx12g via ConfigMap if heap was < 8g

sections.title: Permanent Fix
steps: Root cause: object reference leak in DicomReceiverThread.java — thread pool not releasing DICOM dataset objects
Fix: implement explicit dataset.clear() in DicomReceiverThread finally block
Code fix tracked in ServiceNow CHG-8821 — raise Problem Record PRB-AG-00018 if second occurrence
Post-fix validation: 72h monitoring window — heap should stabilise below 40%

articles.title: HL7 Gateway — Consumer Thread Deadlock Recovery

sections.title: Detection Pattern
steps: Datadog alert: hl7_queue_depth > 1000 — consumer threads not draining queue
Thread dump shows BLOCKED threads waiting on DB connection pool lock
HL7 success rate drops below 95% — normal is 99.9%+
Patient ADT/ORM message processing stops — queue grows at 200-400 msg/min

sections.title: Recovery Steps
steps: HiTL approval required — risk score 34 due to patient data flow impact
Gateway restart: kubectl rollout restart deployment/hl7-gateway
Queue replay: ZeroOps Remediation Agent auto-replays queued messages after restart
Validate: HL7 success rate returns to 99.9%+ within 5 minutes of restart

sections.title: Prevention
steps: Configure DB connection pool max to 50 (current: 20 — insufficient for peak load)
Add deadlock detection timeout: 30 seconds with auto-restart on detection
Scale gateway to 2 replicas during morning peak (06:00-10:00 window)

sections.title: Alert Thresholds
steps: Warning alert at 80% — triggers Capacity Planner forecast review
Critical alert at 90% — triggers emergency expansion planning
Ingest failure at 95% — immediate intervention required
Current ingest rate: 2.4 TB/day — plan for 30-day rolling capacity

sections.title: Emergency Expansion
steps: Azure Blob tier migration for studies > 2 years (immediate 15-20% capacity recovery)
NFS mount expansion via Azure NetApp Files — 4h SLA
Temporary ingest throttle if partition > 93% — prevents write failures

sections.title: Detection
steps: Dynatrace alert: rubee_thread_pool_utilisation > 80%
Worklist distribution latency > 5 minutes — normal is < 30 seconds
Morning peak window 07:00-09:30 is highest risk — plan accordingly

sections.title: Resolution
steps: Apply auto-scale config: maxWorkers 200 → 400 (requires approval — risk score 28)
Restart RUBEE Orchestrator during low-load window if thread leak suspected
Permanent fix: enable Kubernetes HPA for RUBEE deployment — scales on CPU > 70%

sections.title: Root Cause
steps: Inactive replication slot accumulating WAL — common after maintenance windows
Check: SELECT slot_name, active FROM pg_replication_slots — look for active=false
WAL accumulation causes disk pressure and replication lag simultaneously

sections.title: Resolution
steps: Drop inactive slot: SELECT pg_drop_replication_slot('slot_name')
Trigger CHECKPOINT to flush WAL backlog
Monitor replication lag: should return to < 5 seconds within 10 minutes
Re-create slot only if replica genuinely needs it — confirm with DBA

sections.title: Risk Score Thresholds
steps: Risk 0-25 (Pillar 1 Sentinel): fully autonomous — no human gate
Risk 26-50 (Pillar 2 Guardian): one HiTL approval via ServiceNow — 5 min window
Risk 51-100 (Pillar 3 Advisor): AI-assisted human decision — full brief prepared
All actions logged, reversible, and auditable in ServiceNow

sections.title: AGFA-Specific Rules
steps: Never auto-restart HL7 Gateway without HiTL — patient data flow requires approval
PACS rolling restart is auto-approved (risk 18) — heap dump always captured first
VNA storage changes always require HiTL — data integrity risk
AI Inference Engine GPU restart auto-approved after 72h uptime (risk 22)
